User Story: As a visitor, I want to sign in with just my email so I don't need a password
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): /login posts to sendMagicLink; validates email regex, lowercases/trims, then signIn('resend', redirectTo:/entry); shows 'Sending…' then redirects to /login/check-email
Retest (Phase 4): BLOCKED: magic-link delivery needs live Resend

User Story: As an operator, I want sign-in abuse-limited so my email/Resend reputation is safe
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): sendMagicLink counts loginAttempts per email (max 3) and per IP (max 10) in a 10-min window; over limit returns friendly error and does not send
Verification Method: Static trace; count logic exercised via PGlite

User Story: As a user entering a bad email, I want a clear error
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): EMAIL_RE rejects malformed input; returns 'Enter a valid email address.'

User Story: As a user, after requesting a link I see a confirmation page
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): /login/check-email renders 'Check your email' card

User Story: As a signed-in user, I can sign out
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): SignOutButton -> signOutAction -> signOut(redirectTo:/)

User Story: As a visitor vs member, the nav reflects my state
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): AuthNav: no session -> 'Sign in'; session -> 'My entry'; admin email -> '+Admin'

User Story: As the auth system, magic-link callbacks are handled
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): /api/auth/[...nextauth] mounts NextAuth handlers (Resend provider, JWT, Drizzle adapter)

User Story: As a signed-in user, I can create my entry profile
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): /entry requires session (redirect /login); ProfileForm -> saveProfile validates displayName (required, <=60), upserts entrant by normalized email

User Story: As a user, I can edit my profile until lock
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): saveProfile blocked when isLocked(); pre-lock upsert updates row

User Story: As a user, I build a 5-player lineup via typeahead
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): PlayerPicker fetches /players.json, filters by searchName substring client-side, max 8 results, add up to 5, remove

User Story: As a user, my lineup must be exactly 5 distinct valid players
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): saveLineup: isLocked guard; requires profile; Set size==5 and length==5; getPlayersByIds returns 5; else error

User Story: As a user, my submission time is set once and not reset by edits
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): replacePicks sets submittedAt via coalesce(submittedAt, now()) so the tie-break clock is fixed at first confirm

User Story: As a user, after lock my entry is frozen and read-only
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): saveProfile/saveLineup return locked error; /entry post-lock shows read-only final lineup

User Story: As a late user with no entry, after lock I'm told entries closed
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): /entry post-lock with no entrant -> 'Entries are closed' card

User Story: As a returning user, my saved picks prefill the editor
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): getEntrantPickIds -> PlayerPicker initialPlayerIds; resolved against players.json
Errors Found: Picks whose player_id is missing from players.json are silently dropped (user sees <5, blocked on save with generic error)

User Story: As anyone, I can view the public scoreboard
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): /scoreboard ISR (revalidate 60); pre-lock reads submitted entrants; post-lock reads precomputed leaderboard
Errors Found: Pre-lock list read the cron-populated leaderboard, so a just-submitted user did not appear for up to 24h
Fix Status (Phase 3): FIXED: pre-lock reads entrants table directly (getEnteredEntrants)
Retest (Phase 4): PASS (integration: submitted entrant appears with no compute)

User Story: As a viewer pre-lock, I see who entered but no totals/picks
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): Pre-lock rows show display name + 'Entered' badge; no totals, no links

User Story: As a viewer post-lock, I see totals, state and rank
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): Post-lock rows show total/21, StatePill, rank, and link to /entrants/[id]

User Story: As a viewer post-lock, I can open an entrant's lineup
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): /entrants/[id]: notFound() pre-lock; post-lock shows lineup with per-player season TDs

User Story: As a viewer pre-lock, entrant pages stay private
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): /entrants/[id] calls notFound() when !isLocked()

User Story: As a viewer, I can see a player's weekly TD log and season total
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): /players/[id]: getPlayer filters playerWeekStats by currentSeason(); weekly rush/rec rows + total

User Story: As a viewer post-lock, I can see who picked a player
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): getPlayerPickers only called when isLocked(); 'Picked by' card hidden pre-lock

User Story: As a viewer, I can browse all teams
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): /teams (revalidate 3600) lists distinct non-null team codes
Retest (Phase 4): BLOCKED: query not in integration set (low risk)

User Story: As a viewer, I can see a team's roster and totals
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): /teams/[team] upcases code; getTeamPlayers returns roster sorted by season total desc; notFound if empty
Errors Found: Card heading misrepresented the roster (claimed scorers-only)
Fix Status (Phase 3): FIXED: heading changed to 'Roster — non-passing TDs this season'

User Story: As the game, a lineup's total is the sum of 5 players' non-passing TDs
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): scoreLineup sums nonPassingTd (rush_td+rec_td upstream)

User Story: As the game, lineup state is invalid/short/blackjack/bust
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): scoreLineup: any 0 -> invalid; >21 bust; ==21 blackjack; else short (20=short,22=bust)

User Story: As the game, only valid non-bust lineups can win; ties -> earliest submit
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): rankEntrants: pool=valid non-bust; sort by total desc then submittedAt asc

User Story: As the game, if every valid lineup busts, lowest bust wins
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): rankEntrants fallback: pool=valid busts; sort total asc then submittedAt; invalid excluded

User Story: As the game, weekly NFL TD stats are ingested from Sleeper
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): fetchWeekStats hits Sleeper stats endpoint; maps rush_td/rec_td; ingestWeek upserts
Retest (Phase 4): PASS (integration: ingestWeek via stubbed Sleeper payload)

User Story: As the game, unknown players don't abort ingestion
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): ingestWeek filters scoring to ids present in players (FK-safe)
Retest (Phase 4): PASS (integration: unknown id + team row dropped)

User Story: As the game, rescinded TDs are corrected downward
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): ingestWeek deletes rows for the week whose player is no longer scoring

User Story: As the game, re-running ingestion is safe (idempotent)
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): onConflictDoUpdate on (player,season,week) overwrites, never accumulates

User Story: As the game, the leaderboard is precomputed from picks+stats
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): computeLeaderboard: season-scoped totals, builds rankable (submitted & 5 picks), upserts leaderboard

User Story: As Vercel cron, a daily refresh runs securely
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): /api/cron/refresh-stats requires Bearer CRON_SECRET; ingests season + recomputes

User Story: As an admin, I can refresh stats on demand
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): /admin RefreshButton -> refreshNow (requireAdmin) -> ingest + recompute + revalidate
Retest (Phase 4): PASS (integration: ingest+compute path); admin gate needs session

User Story: As an admin, only allowlisted emails can access /admin
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): isAdminEmail(env ADMIN_EMAILS, lowercased); page redirects non-admins; actions re-check requireAdmin

User Story: As an admin, I see entrant/submission counts
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): getAdminStats: count(*) and count(submittedAt)

User Story: As an admin, I can see the lock state and config
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): /admin shows Locked/Open + lock timestamp, or a loud warning when LOCK_AT unset

User Story: As an admin, I can review and triage feedback
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): listFeedback newest-first; FeedbackStatusSelect -> updateFeedbackStatus (requireAdmin)

User Story: As a user, I can submit feedback from any page
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): FeedbackForm posts message + pathname context -> submitFeedback inserts row

User Story: As an operator, feedback is rate-limited against spam
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): Cookie cooldown (UX) + server-side DB check by email/IP in 60s window

User Story: As an operator, I'm emailed new feedback
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): sendFeedbackDigest via Resend when AUTH_RESEND_KEY + FEEDBACK_NOTIFY_EMAIL set; no-op otherwise

User Story: As a user, one lineup per email regardless of casing
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): Email normalized (trim+lowercase) at query boundary; unique(email)

User Story: As a player, I get 5 random teams each visit
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): /play force-dynamic; shuffle(listTeams) Fisher-Yates; picks first 5 teams with >=1 eligible scorer

User Story: As a player, I pick one player per team with totals hidden
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): PlayPicker: spin animation, choose() records pick, totals not shown until reveal

User Story: As a player, the reveal scores my 5 picks vs 21 using 2025 finals
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): revealPlayLineup validates 5 distinct valid ids; getFinalSeasonTotals(2025); scoreLineup

User Story: As a player, blackjack triggers confetti
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): handleReveal: dynamic import canvas-confetti when state==blackjack

User Story: As a player, I can play again with fresh teams
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): playAgain resets state and router.refresh()

User Story: As a visitor, the home page explains the game with a CTA
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): / static hero + example lineup + how-it-works; CTAs to /entry and /scoreboard

User Story: As a visitor, I can read the full rules
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): /rules static rule list matching scoring spec

User Story: As a visitor, I can read the privacy note
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): /privacy static content

User Story: As a mobile user, nav collapses into a hamburger
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): MobileNav md:hidden toggle + dropdown; inline nav hidden md:flex

User Story: As a visitor, the footer shows a JustGiving CTA only when configured
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): Footer justGivingUrl={env}; hidden when unset

User Story: As a user hitting an error, I see a friendly recoverable page
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): app/error.tsx logs + 'Try again' (reset); not-found.tsx themed 404

User Story: As a crawler, robots/sitemap are served
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): app/robots.ts + app/sitemap.ts

User Story: As a user, responses carry security headers
Expected Behaviour (derived from code): next.config headers: XFO DENY, nosniff, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy, CSP
